paragraphs.text: Diagnostic workflows for the four most commonly reported failure modes when operating FerroFlux gateways and embedded sensor fleets. Each section opens with a symptom table, followed by ordered resolution steps and the exact commands required to verify a fix.

Connection failures manifest when a registered device cannot establish or maintain a session with the gateway broker. The middleware will surface these in the device.session stream with non-zero exit codes.
Probe the broker's TLS listener from the device subnet. A healthy broker returns the server certificate chain within 2s.
Use fluxctl diag sessions to enumerate the last 50 session transitions for a device. Look for a repeated HANDSHAKE_FAIL reason.
If the certificate is within 14 days of expiry, rotate using the SDK's renewal helper. The device will reconnect automatically once the new cert lands in its keystore.
Set keepalive_secs in the device profile to at least 2× the observed network RTT. Default is 30; raise to 60–90 for cellular fleets.
Every reading pushed into FerroFlux is validated against the device's declared schema. Mismatches are rejected at ingest and never reach downstream streams.
Confirm which schema revision the gateway is enforcing for this device.
Validate a sample reading against the schema without sending it.
Wrap legacy readings using the compatibility shim until firmware can be reflashed.
End-to-end latency is measured from device.ts to ingest.ts. The platform SLO is <800ms at the 95th percentile.
In the device's .flux profile, drop flush_threshold from 256 to 32 readings to trade throughput for freshness.
Force a chrony resync and verify drift is under 200ms.
FerroFlux authenticates devices via mutual TLS and operators via short-lived JWTs issued by /auth/token.
Remove the entry from the revocation list and re-issue a certificate. The device must reboot to clear cached rejection state.

When opening a support ticket, include a full fluxctl diag bundle archive. The bundle contains 24h of broker logs, device session traces, schema cache state, and the active configuration — all redacted of secrets.

Attach the resulting tarball to your ticket along with the affected device IDs and the approximate UTC timestamp of the first observed failure. Support response targets are 4 business hours for CRITICAL and 1 business day for everything else.

items.text:
Rotate the operator signing key.
          
            bash
fluxctl auth rotate-key --kid "ops-2024-q3"

Verify the device fingerprint.
          
            bash
fluxctl devices show "edge-007" | grep fingerprint
# Compare to expected SHA-256 in your CMDB

Re-enroll if revoked in error.
          Remove the entry from the revocation list and re-issue a certificate. The device must reboot to clear cached rejection state.

        Support Portal

SCHEMA_FIELD_MISSING
            Required field absent from payload; check schema version pinning.
            HIGH

SCHEMA_TYPE_MISMATCH
            Floating-point sent where integer expected (or inverse).
            MEDIUM

SCHEMA_LEGACY_V1 DEPRECATED
            Device still emitting v1 envelopes. Migrate before SDK 3.0.
            MEDIUM

SCHEMA_RANGE_VIOLATION
            Numeric reading outside min/max declared range.
            LOW

401 invalid_signature
            Operator JWT signed with rotated key.
            HIGH

403 device_revoked
            Device fingerprint present in revocation list.
            CRITICAL

AUTH_BASIC_HEADER DEPRECATED
            Basic auth removed in v2.2. Migrate to bearer tokens.
            HIGH

429 too_many_logins
            Login rate-limit; back off for 60s.
            LOW

ECONNREFUSED on port 8443
            Broker process not listening or firewall dropping inbound TLS.
            CRITICAL

handshake_timeout (30s)
            Mismatched TLS cipher suites or expired client certificate.
            HIGH

flapping reconnects > 5/min
            Keepalive interval shorter than network MTU round-trip.
            MEDIUM

SESSION_REJECTED_FULL
            Broker session pool exhausted; raise max_sessions.
            LOW

p95 latency > 2s sustained
            Batch buffer too large; reduce flush_threshold.
            HIGH

clock_skew > 5s
            Device NTP drift; falls back to broker timestamp.
            MEDIUM

backpressure_dropped
            Ingest queue saturated; horizontally scale broker shards.
            CRITICAL

visibleText:
Ferro Flux / docs SDK v2.4.1 · stable Documentation Getting Started API Reference Configuration DSL Changelog Troubleshooting On This Page Connection Failures Schema Validation High Latency Auth Rejections Diagnostic Logs Resources Rust SDK Docs DSL Grammar Status Page Support Portal docs / troubleshooting Troubleshooting Diagnostic workflows for the four most commonly reported failure modes when operating FerroFlux gateways and embedded sensor fleets. Each section opens with a symptom table, followed by ordered resolution steps and the exact commands required to verify a fix. Before you begin. Confirm your gateway is running SDK v2.3.0 or later. Many of the diagnostic commands below depend on the fluxctl diag subcommand introduced in 2.3. 1. Connection Failures Connection failures manifest when a registered device cannot establish or maintain a session with the gateway broker. The middleware will surface these in the device.session stream with non-zero exit codes. Symptom Likely Cause Severity ECONNREFUSED on port 8443 Broker process not listening or firewall dropping inbound TLS. CRITICAL handshake_timeout (30s) Mismatched TLS cipher suites or expired client certificate. HIGH flapping reconnects > 5/min Keepalive interval shorter than network MTU round-trip. MEDIUM SESSION_REJECTED_FULL Broker session pool exhausted; raise max_sessions . LOW Resolution Steps Verify the broker is reachable. Probe the broker's TLS listener from the device subnet. A healthy broker returns the server certificate chain within 2s. bash # From the device-side network namespace openssl s_client -connect broker.flux.local: 8443 \
  -servername broker.flux.local -showcerts < /dev/null Inspect the session log. Use fluxctl diag sessions to enumerate the last 50 session transitions for a device. Look for a repeated HANDSHAKE_FAIL reason. bash fluxctl diag sessions --device "edge-007" --tail 50 Rotate the device certificate. If the certificate is within 14 days of expiry, rotate using the SDK's renewal helper. The device will reconnect automatically once the new cert lands in its keystore. rust use ferroflux::auth::CertRenewal; let renewal = CertRenewal:: new (&ctx)
    . with_csr_path ( "/etc/flux/csr.pem" )
    . execute ()
    . await ?; println! ( "renewed: {}" , renewal.fingerprint); Tune keepalive. Set keepalive_secs in the device profile to at least 2× the observed network RTT. Default is 30; raise to 60–90 for cellular fleets. 2. Schema Validation Errors Every reading pushed into FerroFlux is validated against the device's declared schema. Mismatches are rejected at ingest and never reach downstream streams. Symptom Likely Cause Severity SCHEMA_FIELD_MISSING Required field absent from payload; check schema version pinning. HIGH SCHEMA_TYPE_MISMATCH Floating-point sent where integer expected (or inverse). MEDIUM SCHEMA_LEGACY_V1 DEPRECATED Device still emitting v1 envelopes. Migrate before SDK 3.0. MEDIUM SCHEMA_RANGE_VIOLATION Numeric reading outside min/max declared range. LOW Resolution Steps Print the active schema. Confirm which schema revision the gateway is enforcing for this device. bash fluxctl schema show --device "edge-007" --format json Dry-run a payload locally. Validate a sample reading against the schema without sending it. bash fluxctl schema validate \
  --schema sensors/temp_v3.flux \
  --payload '{"ts":1719283200,"c":21.4}' Migrate from v1 envelopes. Wrap legacy readings using the compatibility shim until firmware can be reflashed. rust use ferroflux::compat::v1_envelope; let reading = v1_envelope:: lift (legacy_bytes)?;
client. push ( "streams/temp" , &reading). await ?; 3. High Latency Readings End-to-end latency is measured from device.ts to ingest.ts . The platform SLO is <800ms at the 95th percentile. Symptom Likely Cause Severity p95 latency > 2s sustained Batch buffer too large; reduce flush_threshold . HIGH clock_skew > 5s Device NTP drift; falls back to broker timestamp. MEDIUM backpressure_dropped Ingest queue saturated; horizontally scale broker shards. CRITICAL Resolution Steps Inspect ingest latency histogram. bash fluxctl diag latency --window 15m --bucket p50,p95,p99 Lower the device flush threshold. In the device's .flux profile, drop flush_threshold from 256 to 32 readings to trade throughput for freshness. flux profile "edge-fast" {
  flush_threshold = 32 flush_interval  = "500ms" compression     = "zstd" } Resync NTP. Force a chrony resync and verify drift is under 200ms. bash chronyc -a makestep chronyc tracking | grep "System time" 4. Authentication Rejections FerroFlux authenticates devices via mutual TLS and operators via short-lived JWTs issued by /auth/token . Symptom Likely Cause Severity 401 invalid_signature Operator JWT signed with rotated key. HIGH 403 device_revoked Device fingerprint present in revocation list. CRITICAL AUTH_BASIC_HEADER DEPRECATED Basic auth removed in v2.2. Migrate to bearer tokens. HIGH 429 too_many_logins Login rate-limit; back off for 60s. LOW Deprecation notice. Basic authentication headers will return 401 in SDK v3.0 . Issue bearer tokens via POST /auth/token and pass them in Authorization: Bearer … . Resolution Steps Rotate the operator signing key. bash fluxctl auth rotate-key --kid "ops-2024-q3" Verify the device fingerprint. bash fluxctl devices show "edge-007" | grep fingerprint # Compare to expected SHA-256 in your CMDB Re-enroll if revoked in error. Remove the entry from the revocation list and re-issue a certificate. The device must reboot to clear cached rejection state. Collecting Diagnostic Logs When opening a support ticket, include a full fluxctl diag bundle archive. The bundle contains 24h of broker logs, device session traces, schema cache state, and the active configuration — all redacted of secrets. bash # Generate a redacted diagnostic bundle fluxctl diag bundle \
  --since "24h" \
  --include sessions,schema,config,metrics \
  --redact secrets,pii \
  --output "/tmp/flux-diag-$(date +%s).tgz" Attach the resulting tarball to your ticket along with the affected device IDs and the approximate UTC timestamp of the first observed failure. Support response targets are 4 business hours for CRITICAL and 1 business day for everything else. Tip. You can stream live diagnostic events to your terminal with fluxctl diag tail --level=warn . Useful while reproducing intermittent issues. © 2024 FerroFlux Systems · Docs build 7c4e1f9 Edit this page Report an issue Status On This Page Connection Failures Schema Validation Errors High Latency Readings Authentication Rejections Collecting Diagnostic Logs
